reviews.review_text:
Genuinely the biggest waste of money and most disappointing game I've ever played. 90% of the development of this game was put into the giant open world aspect and everything else is cut or neutered entirely in service of that. How dumb to make a video game about the 9 Star Wars movies and have said movies be a background filler element of the game. It's so boring and lame. The story is 90% just walking around and going from point A to point B in the open world. Occasionally you will get a level that will typically take you genuinely like a minute or two to beat. The levels sometimes no joke will just be like walk down a hallway, kill 10 droids, you're done congrats. The boss fights are all the same and get boring after you do one. There's no replay-ability because like I said the story mode is genuinely 90% walking to different parts of the map. You can only replay the tiny 2 minute missions that are brainless and not fun. The story mode is legit so unfun to play through it's remarkable like I don't think I've ever seen worse level design in a game before. No hub world, instead you have a cluttered and terrible UI. No character creation. There are 380 characters which sounds incredible and impressive at first until you realize they're all copies of each other. Instead of making unique characters they made a class system and just throw each character into a class to make it easier to reach that 380 goal post. Every bounty hunter is the same. The droids are the same. The Jedi are the same. The Sith are the same. SOMETIMES there are very slight alterations between characters in classes like maybe they'll have a unique(ish) weapon but that is it. R2D2 can't even fly, nice. Same goes for the DLC characters, just copies of other characters and thrown into classes and they called it a day. Why even have so many characters without a character creator honestly.

Well I said 90% of the development clearly went into the open world so is that good. I guess. Each world is really detailed and ginormous and fun to explore. Unfortunately, most planets are locked behind completing the segments of the story mode that they are introduced in so I didn't even see most of the worlds in the game because I didn't even make it through the entire prequel trilogy before I was bored to death and couldn't finish this joke of a game. I did unlock a good chunk of worlds though still and I played through them extensively since there is nothing to do other than meander around these worlds. Is that fun though? In my opinion, eh, not really? I mean yeah the worlds are huge and super detailed and amazing and I had fun for a little exploring them. It gets old though EXTREMELY FAST. Because like what's the point? Thousands of collectibles in these giant open worlds but what is supposed to motivate me to try to get these collectibles. You could probably 100% the complete sage in LESS THAN HALF the time you could in this game if I had to guess. But in complete sage you'd have to do that by replaying the levels of your favorite Star Wars movies with tons of fun, unique and different characters. You'd have to bring a team of Chewbacca, R2D2, 4-LOM, Luminara Unduli, Yoda force ghost and Palpatine to solve puzzles you previously had to skip when you played through the game the first time. This game, you're not doing that. No instead you'll fly back to Geonosis later when you unlock the first of a certain class of character, use that character to unlock an area and congrats collectible acquired. Riveting stuff man. There are some puzzles but they are nothing burgers compared to what we use to have in the complete sage. It's baffling because Lego Clone Wars took the franchise in such a great direction with having a kind of open world hub world and still having the amazing lego levels, and they just threw that out entirely.

Boring and absurdly disappointing game. Don't let the screenshots and amazing looking worlds fool you. You will get bored of this game so fast and never even bother to explore all the worlds I guarantee you.

1. no replayability
2. insane time sink that doesnt feel anywhere near as rewarding or as fun as lego star wars the complete saga (2009) an extreme amount of content over the complete saga, but infinitely more boring
3. go from point a to point b ad infinitum
4. several hundred characters that pretty much all do the same exact thing
5. empty open world hubs that further emphasize the fact that you are playing a walking simulator
6. a thousand collectibles to find, a staggering amount of side content, but its all boring and not rewarding.
7. too much inspiration from ubisoft's boring, empty and repetitive open world games
8. is travelers tales going to be the next ubislop?
9. like dawg fr it feels as if this game is trying to compete with warframe in terms of being one of the most drawn out and repetitive games ever made

The Skywalker Saga was very enjoyable! Gameplay was different to other Lego games I've played and was refreshing. Graphics are really nice especially the reflections and texture details close up, while also running great without any hiccups. 

Many silly jokes and puns befitting a Lego game. I don't know much about Star Wars but it's peaked my interest in the Star Wars franchise.

Would recommend!
The Kids love playing this one, Tho can be hard to get them off it lol. the game itself run great and no bugs so far.

genuinely might be the laziest lego game ive ever played. its all filler, horribly boring and dull vehicle levels. and most of the game play is walking to a tiny boring level. the same puzzles over and over. also getting a darkside character at the start of the game? where is the substance beside the visuals. this is genuinly dreadful. i love the lego games but this isnt a lego game there's no fun besides the cutscenes no fun exciting animations or puzzles. the same lightsaber battles over and over. compare it to other lego games and you'll see how little substance this game has. also no custom characters. why is there no lego game in this lego game??????

Tried loving this game but the more I played it the more I hated it. Horrifically bland level designs with all focus being put into the open world content (which isn't even superb). Quantity over quality.

Honestly this game is just baffling in a lot of ways. The open world is fun, and your first playthrough of each episode is great but when replaying it, you realize how much of each level is just the open world parts, which can't be replayed for some godforsaken reason. The combat system feels really out of place, and though it's kind of fun in a stupid way (air comboing an unkillable character across huge gaps is amusing), it's not at all engaging. I don't like the move to the close, over the shoulder camera they landed on for this game. The original LEGO Star Wars and the following games had a camera system I liked, but if this game followed the more distant LEGO Marvel camera, for example, then I'd have few issues with it. However, the new camera just doesn't feel like LEGO to me. Moreover, they never added a character creator. I never played the sequel trilogy of this game, but that was largely because I was just so frustrated with a lot of the choices this game made. The original and Lego Clone Wars are better.

When you get to play a level its not half bad, but it takes 20 minutes of running around empty "open world" talking to npc's before you actually get to each level. Not every game needs to be some grand open world that you are expected to put hundreds of hours into, this game lacks the charm and ease of play of the original LEGO games that were so iconic.

LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga was a fantastic trip down memory lane for me. The only LEGO Star Wars game I had played before was one of the original trilogy titles on a handheld years ago, so jumping into a game that covers all nine films felt like experiencing the entire saga for the first time. What immediately stood out was how well TT Games balanced nostalgia with fresh ideas. The story follows the movies, but not in a scene-for-scene recreation. Instead, it injects the classic LEGO humor into nearly every moment, with goofy reactions, visual gags, and reimagined scenes that make even familiar moments feel new. Whether you're a lifelong Star Wars fan or just enjoy the LEGO games, it's hard not to smile at some of the ridiculous situations the game creates.

The amount of content here is honestly staggering. With 45 story levels, 24 planets to explore, more than 400 playable characters, countless ships, side quests, collectibles, minikits, Kyber Bricks, and optional challenges, there is always something to work toward. The controls felt smooth whether using a keyboard or controller, and the updated combat system adds a little more depth than previous LEGO games without becoming complicated. Visually, this is easily one of the best-looking LEGO titles ever made. The environments are detailed, the planets feel alive, and the LEGO aesthetic has never looked better. It also runs extremely well and goes on sale frequently, making it an easy recommendation for anyone interested in the franchise.

That said, the game's biggest weakness is repetition. When you're chasing 100% completion, you'll start to notice certain activities being reused quite often. Space battles, collectible hunts, and some side quests can begin to blend together after dozens of hours. There are also occasional bugs that can interfere with level progression or prevent challenges from completing correctly, sometimes requiring a restart to fix. Thankfully, these issues never outweighed the fun for me. The humor, variety of locations, massive roster, and sheer amount of content make this one of the strongest LEGO games available today. It's not perfect, but it's the kind of game that's easy to lose yourself in for hours while reliving some of Star Wars' most iconic moments through a wonderfully silly LEGO lens.

—The Ember Archives
https://store.steampowered.com/curator/45601239/

I just can't recommend this game. I'm tired of pretending it doesn't suck when it's super flawed

When people compare this game to older titles like 'The Complete Saga' or 'The Clone Wars'. It's really easy to point that out and say, "this is just you being blinded by nostalgia" and to a certain extent I agree. But genuinely, you are better off with any other Star Wars game that isn't this one. I mean it

This game is just genuinely so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ boring. It deserves to be compared to older titles because they offer way more story-driven content compared to this game.

The biggest con is that it focuses WAY more on side-missions & open world content rather than it does the movies themselves. This can work but it's a double edged sword considered just how little effort was put into playing the movies. Another huge gripe that I have is that you can't even replay movies, so if you want to replay a story or any movies, you need to create a whole new save file. 

I won't complain about any optimization or poor performance, because I actually ran this game perfectly with max graphics - which I will admit, was pure eye candy. Everything looks so good in this game. Neither will I go after the dialogue, since I also think it's decent & not cringe-inducing or annoying. It's whimsical like the other titles, except with actual words instead of mumbling.

People usually hold the 'No custom character' as a standing point as to why this game isn't as good but my biggest letdowns were:

The UI. If you're gonna play this on PC, get ready for some of the worst UI work ever done by a triple-A studio. It is really, really bad. And sloppy. The inclusion of a tech-tree as well is just completely unnecessary, as well as the removal of a central hub-world. All you're going to get is free-play, storymode, and the main menu.

Lack of genuine large-scale space battles (Which is so egregious given just how important they were to the story). They have the capability to do this, I don't know why they skimped out on it. Don't download this game expecting an epic battle over Coruscant or the Forest Moon of Endor. The most you're gonna get is just unlocking Capital Ships, & even then it is just you versus one ship and maybe a few enemies.

The super short missions. Yes, they are genuinely so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ short, I'm not saying it to hate. They really are insanely short, every single one of them. They try to draw it out with some scavenging or other types of puzzles, where you maybe go around and get a few items, or go around solving a few puzzles. Or both - but again, you will go into a mission, fight enemies, and finish it within a very unsatisfying amount of time. 

Nothing is taken serious. Yes, I know this game is for kids. But they seriously dumbed down a lot of topics within the story. Everything is far more sanitized to keep a family rating, which isn't terrible & makes sense given the way Star Wars is viewed by a lot of people - especially families.. but seriously, you will not see the proper, respected (usually made into slapstick) versions of tragedies within Star Wars. Which, I mean, comeon. It's okay to take things seriously every once in a while, everyone can handle it but I suppose the developers can't. This game is literally for man-children.

The most EGREGIOUS example being [spoiler]the destruction of Alderaan.[/spoiler]

But yeah. I just can't play this crap. It's boring, repetitive, and offers not enough of value to stay installed. 

If you want to play Star Wars movies but in LEGO form, don't play this. You have better options.
If you want an open-world game that offers a lot of SIDE-QUESTS, collectibles, characters, and whatnot to gradually collect overtime, then cool- this game's for you.
have only put about a day of hours and it feel like a chore to 100% the game, the story it great but how long the game is for 100% isnt fun

By far my least favorite of all the LEGO games I have played
Despite being one of the newer ones it really doesn't look like it, and they over complexed a lot of the gameplay that was so simple before

I have loved this game since it's release. I've got it on every platform, even Switch. The one thing that will forever tarnish this otherwise amazing game for me is that TT delayed this game for over a year and when it launched during COVID time, it has no online multiplayer. That insanity to me.

Other than that, it's amazing.

This game gives me mixed feelings.
My first ever video game was Lego Star Wars the complete Saga so I was really excited when the Skywalker Saga came around.
And I have to say, graphically, the most beautiful Lego Star Wars game. The massive open world is one of the most complete Star Wars experiences that you can get.
However, the open world is also the worst part of the game. All the quests are just too much. Too many tedious missions that are not well designed.
I wouldn't have minded it so much if the story levels were at least nice, but they were way too short and rushed. Less is more.
They should have taken more time fully fleshing out all the levels instead of bloating the open world.
I hope Lego fixes these issues in their next Lego Star Wars game.
this might be the most mediocre LEGO game ever made. play the complete saga or the clone wars lego games instead, they are much more fun.
Genuinely a bad experience. Words cannot fanthom how poor of an experience this was. A 70 dollar game and this is what it is? Horrible.
LOVE IT! I completed the OG SW lego games like so many times on my PS2, so this is just, chef's kiss, 10/10!!!

the games good, some of the jokes aged poorly, but the biggest reason why this is a negative review is because of what isnt there, custom characters, couch co-op, and other features that were just stripped from the game

ive had this game playing in the background (which is why i have play hour) but ive been unable to play a  single level
1st it would just freeze on the menu (giving it hours to load did nothing and now that ive solved the previous issue i cant play the first level because the camera sensitivity is so high that even touching the mouse causes it to spin wildly. ive adjusted my mouse settings and the games camera setting neither work.
This Game is awful, 90 percent of the game is just walking the the missions which take around 5 minates, you should get the complete saga instead.

Honestly lacks the soul of The Complete Saga. Levels are noticeably short, even compared to that game. While the roster and gameplay are nice, thats about it. Level design sucks. Id rather play Complete Saga and LSW 3
only reason i dont recommend the game is because it doesnt have online multiplayer despite releasing in 2022

I write reviews for games, and I try to make them meaningful. I think I can keep this one simple, though, and still have meaning:
Is it Lego? Yes. Is it Star Wars? Yes. Is it funny? Yes. Can you hit everyone and (nearly) everything with your stabby lightsaber? Yes! That is all the boxes checked for me, great game!

i Don't even Really like Star wars, So i Have no Idea what's Going on In this Game
laser Swords are Fun to Use, and Shooting people With laser Guns is Also very Fun
another Game where Red guy Thinks it's A game For children

it's Still fun Though. . . 

7.5/10

- dim

Story:
Like in the original LEGO Star Wars, this is an abridged retelling of the previous 6 episodes along with the new Sequel Trilogy, with some gags and in-jokes and memes thrown in. Unlike the original 2 games and their complete edition, the entire movie is redone even if each episode only has 5 levels, every important plot point is in the game including Anakin killing the Tusken Raiders or Yoda vs Palpatine to name some examples. Though they did cut main villain Tarkin from “A New Hope” but kept the old First Order guy from “RoS”

All the characters are talking now which I don’t mind though I think some of the jokes are a bit too fast pace like the death Qui-Gon Jin which I get the joke but went by so quickly. Of course there are just as many jokes that are really good like the reveal that Padme is the queen and there is a follow up to it at least twice.

But the big question is “How does the sequel Trilogy hold up in LEGO?”  Pretty good for the most part. Due to being abridged, most of the fluff got cut or shortened but still keeps the main plot of those films and reminded me why I enjoyed them...Except for Rise of Skywalker. RoS is such a bad story in its core that not even having the LEGO treatment can salvage it. It’s obvious the root of the problem is that Palpatine shows up to take the role of Big Bad from Kylo so he can have an undeserved redemption arc and the story has to rush and bend to make the story about stopping Palpatine and forcing a connection between him and Rey when her just being some kid who was abandoned by her parents was perfect.  And everyone agrees that he ruins the previous movies just by existing.

Also Rey should’ve had a dual blade lightsaber since it fits her fighting style, starts dating BB-8 and she should have been adopted by Chewbacca.
Gameplay:
Lets start with the basic gameplay. There are 7 main classes(okay, there’s actually 9 but the Light and Dark side are the same while Extra is just full of, well, extras) with some skills that overlap like heroes, astromechs and hunters can use grapple hooks and you use these skills to get through the levels and look for bricks, money and other collectibles. But that’s normal for LEGO games so lets talk about the combat. 
Lightsaber is mostly button mashing with the occasional dodge roll and force push to stop a block. And as basic as it is, it’s really fun. It’s fun picking up objects and foes and hurling them at enemies, it’s fun controlling an enemy and blasting their friends. And while most of the bosses are fought the same way, the fights are really good and actually feel like sword fights with the dodging and waiting for openings to strike. 

And then there’s the gun combat which is really good, even when you have a simple blaster, is good  to use and the enemies use and build cover and have a variety of different weapons like mini-guns or rockets. Though if you want the full arsenal, you’ll have to be a villain or wear a disguise to use grenades and to open boxes with the other guns like a machine gun or a flamethrower. And then there’s using your ship to fly around in space and it’s pretty good. Simple to use and the challenges aren’t too hard. Though I did find trying to talk to other ships to be annoying since they kept pushing me away when I got too close and some ships are just not suited or able to do ship combat at all.

Before I move on, I should mention the accessibility options for a bit. Just some basic things like Subtitles, screen effects, mission markers, losing money for fall damage, or health recovers on its own. Just stuff to make the game easier but have the option to turn off. And theres aim assist which makes it easier to shoot at things with lock on features and homing shots. I had half of these settings off since I’m an adult who’s played a good many shooters and aside from that one level where you have a car chase with the assassin which was annoying cause she’s small target and the car’s bullets are weak. But on the other hand, having them off made the dogfight with Jango Fett one of the best boss fights and made me wish there were bosses in space that weren’t battle ships.

But then we get to Endor The Line, which was fine at first but after a while, the road disappears, you fall of the edge of the map and have to restart. Hate to say to say it but this game is really glitchy and crashes a lot. I lost count on how many crashes I had during my play-though and I was worried I wasn’t able to 100% it.

Now I don’t want to end this section on a negative note so lets talk about the levels. There are two types of levels in this game: Story levels which mainly where you have set pieces and bosses fights like the pod race or a stealth level which ends with a fight with a rancor. I was actually surprised on how short these levels are and I don’t mind this. Stops the levels from dragging. Plus most levels (septically in FreePlay mode) have multiple paths you can take like you could take one route that lets you drive a vehicle or you could take another which focus more on shooting. And some are very open like the Hoth level where you can do the level in any order as long as all three sections are done.

And then there are the free-roam levels which reminded me of levels from Super Mario Odyssey. Aside from connecting the story with fetch quests, escort missions and mini bosses, you can find Kyber Bricks and Ships in nooks, cranny's, boxes and puzzles, as well as side missions for new characters like Gonk Droid and evil Gonk Droid. I like these a lot since it though I did find it annoying how you have to go to other planets to do some missions or finish ones on other planets to unlock others I also felt the Scoundrel class got shafted in these free roaming levels as their main gimmick besides having a charge shot is to shot purple object to start chain reactions, and there isn’t anything like that in Free-Roam so Scoundrels are almost redundant in your party.

But what do the Kyber Bricks do in this Collectathon. Collectibles are meant to get players to do challenges and be rewarded with progression but the levels are unlocked as you play the story naturally so what do the Bricks do? Upgrades. At anytime you can buy an upgrade for your class or just in general like making people afraid of the Sith, money rewards for hacking as well as a skip button, basic attack and health upgrades or even C3-PO being able to make a shock wave when he splits in have. One thing I find very considerate is the option to turn off these upgrades if you find the game too easy or find the perk frustrating like the Scoundrels charge shot which I turned off cause I prefer my blaster going pew-pew.

Presentation:
The LEGO models have come a long way since the first games release. Hair is more accurate, outfits are more detailed and you can play as a naked C3-PO now(or is he skin less). 

Environments are good too. They managed to make each location look different even if they have similar themes, like how you can explore two cities on Tatooine which are different with one being filled with narrow streets and the other has more wide open areas. Got some good map design in general like the casino planet has you going uphill till you reach the casino or Threed has a north and south section divided by a river. Though I would say Endor was annoying to navigate since it’s very vertical

7/10
Just finished LSW TCS, and thought it would be fun to see a decade of progress on graphics and gameplay. While not the same thing, the quality overall is orders of magnitude higher.
Great game! Definitely got lost traveling throughout the galaxy as playing my fav characters.

the millenial writing makes the cutscenes agonizing trash, I don't know who could possibly think millenial writing is funny... it totally misses the mark compared to "the complete saga" back in the day... besides that one critique that I have, the game is just about flawless. feels great to play, looks great. a bit too easy maybe, but an utter joy (besides the god awful cut scenes written by someone with absolutely no sense of humor). I mean for real who would think this is funny I mean I think the internet is unanimously roasting this cringe fest rather than laughing at it. corny corny. I think the intended audience is like 6-9 but I assure you, they don't think it's funny either.

Skywalker Saga may be a flawed game, but it's a very fun time, packed with content to the brim, and a love letter to Star Wars fans. There's a lot of ground to explore and a lot of things to collect, and the LEGO humor is really at home here. Some might understandably prefer the Complete Saga, but you shouldn't pass this one up either, especially on sale.
Boots straight to the store page. cant get to gameplay with a controller attached. SAD

I grew up playing LEGO Star Wars TCS, and when I saw this, I wasnt too sure about it, didnt know if it would be as fun as TCS. I was wrong, this games a blast and I like that it covers all 9 movies. Ive seen people complain about how the story levels feel too short, but this game isnt centered around the story levels, unlike TCS, this game is centered around exploring all the different planets and ships across all 9 movies. Now, sometimes it feels grindy, and if you dont like grinding games, this might not be for you, but if you do, this game has so much content to grind. 9/10 would recommend

This is a very large game. Got this when it was on sale for Star Wars day in May for cheap and it's easily going to be my best purchase this year. If you like Lego games, you're going to like this. The open world aspect of the game in between levels is refreshing compared to the Lego Star Wars games of old with new games and gimmicks to keep it fresh.

Around Christmas 2024, I lost an entire week playing this game.  There's always more to do, and new places to explore.  I wandered back after being disappointed with Batman Legacy and it's just as much fun as I remembered.

If you're playing for the first time and starting with Episode I, don't waste a lot of time in the Gungan city.  It's one of the most confusing areas to navigate in the entire game and without unlocking more characters there's a lot you can't do but won't know why.
Game was playable once, tried playing with my girlfriend and trying to hotfix this dumbass crashing issue very frustratintg.
really good game, worth buying deluxe edition. Rearmored Boba Fett is goated early game

This game was incredible! It was a truly amazing experience. I beat this game on May 4th.

All of my nostalgic energy was crying. I'm truly happy right now.

im conflicted. real review for a sec. short, fun, rushed levels. rushed as in it didn't feel loved like the other games. indy 2 levels are slightly worse. slightly. HUMONGOUS open world + space. BUT one downside. too many collectables. this isnt a BAD but VERY VERY VERY tedious from a 100% view. i liked it for what it was and think that the hate isnt deserved at this capacity. 6.8/10. in other words. ts is tcs the sequal of 100%'s

God tier. All the characters and ships you know and love, same goofy lego energy, good puns all around and the absolute mass of puzzles are pretty fun and don't get stale at all. Skill system is really nice and the flying is so fun, most ships and people/things have their own quirks and sounds and have their own attacks too, 
Only thing I didn't like is needing to play a certain episode to unlock the scavenger's abilities to be able to get a lot of the collectables which you can't get until way later. (Unless you start on the Rey episode, but who would know that.)

1. love the game would recommend
2. WATCH ALL THE ORIGNAL STARWARS FILMS FIRST BEFORE PLAYING THE GAME
3. Make sure your PC is pretty good or youll be playing a Laggy mess.

TL;DR: a fun game with some minor hiccups that is a great value when it's on sale for $5-$10.

Too long; did read:

It's a little jank on PC (mostly runs okay, but if you tab in and out for a second monitor or go to a browser, it'll sometimes lose track of what resolution it should be when you come back is the main issue I've had so far), and the keyboard controls are somewhat unintuitive and jump around more than makes sense (to me at least) as you go through menus, but when you're actually running around shooting/lightsabering/force pushing stuff to collect things it plays fine and is fun,

There are well over 1,000 collectables, so there's always something to wander off and do, and with all 9 episodes and a free play mode in one game, it feels like a pretty great value at the $5 sale price I got it for; more than $10-20 would start to feel a little steep but it seems like it goes for $5-$10 with decent regularity so that seems fair.  It looks like there might be some overpriced character DLCs, so you may want to look into that more deeply before pulling the trigger if you are going to try to 100% literally every possible option, but so far that seems so wildly out of reach that it hasn't triggered my completionism drive and I doubt it will.

I see this game as something to drop into and screw around a bit with when I have a few hours to kill rather than something to mainline, so at only ~6-7 hours in (ugh, I know, I'm so sorry, but that's how long I've been playing), I don't know if the gameplay loop would still be fun after the 100-200+ hours it seems likely getting all the blue blocks would take, but I definitely expect to enjoy the ~40 or so hours I anticipate it taking to do the main 9 movies worth of story and some wandering off to grab things that look shiny.
I never really enjoyed the sequels UNTIL NOW. This game does everything right about LEGO and Star Wars.

Im pretty disappointed.
Grew up with Lego Star Wars Part 1 and 2 on the ps2 and have some of the fondest childhood memories playing them with my friends.
The Skywalker Saga just seems so rushed and mindless, it lacks the charm of the old games.
Yes the hub worlds are big, but the levels are so short and the cutscenes are held so short, that one who isnt a star wars fan would have no idea what the game is about.
Compare that with the complete saga, a game where they didnt even had voice lines and they still achieved a more cohersive story telling.

Lego Starwars 2 and 3 are actually so much better than this. I wish I'd bought that for PC instead of this, but it's too late now. This feels... corporate? Almost bleached. I don't think I'll be buying any new lego games in the future if this is what TT games is like now.

It is very cool and the graphics are unbeliveable. I would like it though as The Complete Saga, it is very kiddish, like when qui gon dies its very funny to kids but in the complete saga it is very sad and not funny. this game is still awsome. Thank you

The levels are underwhelming and the game has way too much sequel content if you're going for 100% completion. Is it the definitive LEGO Star Wars game? No. But it is the definitive Star Wars game with LEGOs. I'd only recommend it on sale for $25 or less.

Definitely one of the best lego games I have played. If you love star wars you'll enjoy this one. 100% this game with achievements will take time but its tracking system of stuff helps you so much especially if you buy all the hints of quests, puzzles, challenges etc etc. But if you just wanna play it for fun and not care about anything else then you will still have a good time. I did run into some bugs but the one that had me thinking if 100%ing the game was possible was were my games audio got delayed and at the same time my fps was slowing getting lower and lower until it just shut itself down, Somehow it only happen in Space and that one mission with Obi and Anakin chasing the shape shifter and I don't know what fixed it but it stopped after many tries of validating flies n stuff. Even after all that I had a really great experiance and I definitely recommend this game.
Realy good for star wars lovers like myself and good for introducing kids to star wars before they're old enough to watch it. good comedy, 5 stars.

i had this game on switch where my save file bugged and i was unable to complete a mission, so I couldn't get 100%. Here I am, the fool, again, with another bugged save file after ive put 40 damn hours into it. 40 hours of running from A-B because thats what you do in 99% of the open world missions. 

If you're a completionist like me, and you reeeallly like holding W, then have fun. and COPY YOUR SAVE FILE BETWEEN SESSIONS
Same star wars lego fun, except this game feels extremely expanded, with more open world parts between actual parts of episodes. Love playing through the original trilogy.
very funny cutscenes and fun gameplay always a blast to play

So, I don't have much play time on Steam since I originally played it on Playstation, only bought it on Steam since I saw a mod to add a character creator, since the devs left it out despite being a staple of LEGO games for so long. Unfortunately mod is broken and abandoned, so I wasted my money. Overall, same thing other reviews are saying, pretty visuals, lacking in a lot of the charm most LEGO games have in both story and gameplay, boss fights are a joke, and, as I said before, no character creator. Even after all this time that last fact still pisses me off, like I might've put up with this lackluster game if it had one, but the fact it doesn't is just the final nail in the coffin to take this from lackluster to something I legit wish never got made.

Good game, only 2 things I didn't like was that you couldn't skip all cutscenes and when you have to deliver something through space the WARNING sign stays waaaaaaaaaay to long for no reason before loading in the space battles between transit.
Took me 80 hours to 100% with all achievements.

I probably wouldnt even play this game if you paid me to.

The camera is annoying af, the movement is weird and clunky. Settings are not set to default when you first boot up the game so thats fun, the game wants you to start with episode 7 when you fist boot up the game. At least let me start at the begoinning or act like im supposed to start there. I got th play the game finally after like 8 mins of booting up, cutscenes and other crap. Speaking of cutscenes, they are unskippable. Esc is not the back key unless it is, idk why they change that for some menu windows. Spending an hour on the first lvl innit of itself should be a crime, since when did lego game levels get soo long? Btw i only made it to the point where you meet jarjar. From the point of boot up to the point where i left the game it should have not taken me more than 10 min.

The format for this review was made by the people on the below named website, etc. etc..

Writing this review after I've 100% the game.

If you are a Star Wars, or Lego Star Wars, fan or have a kid you want to enjoy a game with I cannot recommend this enough.

The game follows the old lego recipe of play the mission in story mode then go back in free play with any character you want (out of the nearly 400 or so available characters) and get the collectibles.

There is an open world aspect to it where you roam planets and do secondary missions or solve mini-puzzles to get kyber chystals, ships or characters. Characters and ships have to be bought using studs, which you collect in missions or the open world though some do unlock automatically by simply doing a specific story mission where that character is used.

Characters are split into classes and each class can do one or two things that other classes cannot. For example only Villains can open silver chests, only Scavangers can make net launchers or gliders.

Each class has unlockable and upgradable skills that require kyber crystals. Usually each skill has 3 tiers with tier one requiring 4 crystals, tier 2 8 and tier 3 12 though there are exceptions. There is also a generic skill tree that you will want to fully unlock first because it contains a lot of quality of life stuff.

The story follows the movies, with a few creative liberties for the sake of gameplay but overall it should be known to anyone who has seen the films.



[h2]The good[/h2]

The game takes the old Lego Star Wars formula and improves on it in every conceivable way. Levels are bigger, more varied, there are more characters, characters are voiced, there's space combat and the humor is much better. 

Combat is fun but and while very easy (this is a kid's game after all) it does not feel unsatisfying.

Some of the puzzles are interesting though all of them are rather easy.

Graphics look great for a lego game and the game runs really well on a mid range PC from 2020. The environments and characters are faithfully recreated and the character movement is very fluid.

Controls are great with either controller (is used an Xbox controller) or mouse and keyboard though it really shows that the devs designed the game primarily for the controller.

The main missions are genuinely fun.

The side mission dialogue is goofy and funny.

I have no ecountered any game breaking or crashes.

Very large number of characters and ships to unlock as well as a number of capital ships which can be unlocked through space combat followed by a short boarding level.



[h2]The bad [/h2]

While there are no game breaking bugs the game does have bugs, from the mildly annoying "thing you've unlocked hours ago" still showing up as newly unlocked to the space combat UI glitching out and not showing you which ships are you enemy or capital ships not showing up.

The space combat while fun is kind of pointless beyond unlocking capital ships. Once you have all 8 ships there is no real reason to engage with it at all.

Capital ships provide nothing beyond a few collectibles. You need to have them for 100% but once you've boarded it it does nothing.

While the dialogue is really funny, a lot of the side missions are just fetch quests or busy work.

There are several hundred kyber crystals to collect in the open world but only 12 or so types of puzzle. This means you will be doing the same "shoot 4 targets in 10 seconds" puzzle at least 10 times with the only variation being the placement of the targets.

Some of the main mission challenges are too vague and you can end up missing them by accident, which means you will have to replay the entire level to it. Chapter 8 particularly egregious in this regard.



[h2]Conclusion[/h2]

This is a fun game and I highly recommend it to anyone who is either a Star Wars fan or just wants something to play with their kids/nephews

When the game came out, it was good news because it had been a long time since we'd had a good Lego game. Wandering around the galaxy is a satisfying feeling, and the jokes are really smart. 
But it would have been better if you could really crank up the difficulty level, especially during the boss fights, because they are overall way too easy.

But so far I'd give this game 4 stars, mainly cuz the controls at times are a bit annoying and the jet-pack characters  can't really fly.
Other than that it's a pretty good game.

The Skywalker Saga offers the true Star Wars experience, covering everything from the Prequels and the Originals to the Sequels. And let’s be honest: while the Sequel movies might be trash, they are actually quite fun to play through in this LEGO version.

[b]The Pros:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Planets & Exploration:[/b] The game features over 20 planet hubs filled with quests and collectibles. I played this until the very end, and it took me over 90 hours to reach that 100% completion mark.
[*][b]Gigantic Roster:[/b] There is a massive amount of characters to unlock. While many share similar abilities, it didn't bother me because every character feels great to control.
[*][b]Visuals:[/b] The graphics are stunning. The particle effects are especially impressive—seeing the sand on Tatooine stick to the characters' clothes and bodies looks amazing.
[*][b]New Camera Perspective:[/b] I really prefer the new 3rd-person over-the-shoulder camera. It feels much more direct and modern compared to the older games.
[*][b]Space Combat:[/b] The space battles are a blast. There are tons of ships to fly, and being able to board and explore massive Capital Ships like Star Destroyers is a huge highlight.
[/list]

[b]The Cons:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Short Levels:[/b] The actual story levels are definitely too small. Some missions didn't even last 2-3 minutes. In that regard, the levels in "The Complete Saga" were much better designed.
[*][b]Repetitive Side Content:[/b] While there is a ton to do, many side tasks feel very similar. Completing everything can become a bit of a tedious grind towards the end.
[/list]

[b]Verdict:[/b]
If you are looking for long, highly replayable story levels, you might want to skip this and stick to the original Complete Saga. However, if you want to lose yourself in a wonderful Star Wars world, explore dozens of planets, and enjoy that classic LEGO humor, I highly recommend it. I had a lot of fun for the most part—even if I still think Episodes 7-9 are garbage!
The game files can be corrupted and lost easily.  For a game that includes such time and progress, the potential to lose all of that irretrievably is unacceptable.

Super disappointed with this game. I played many other LEGO games, previous Star Wars especially, and this one is definitely the worst. Voicing characters takes away the vibe, leading the player by hand and showing exactly what to do makes me feel like I'm in forever tutorial. Not recommended.
Amazing game with a lot of quests and content to complete
Good fun, if a little jarring on how it works after playing every other Lego game. But I had a great time with this.

This is a game with a good story mode, but it's not a good Lego game. The story is executed well with some good jokes sprinkled in, the visuals are really good, exploration is fun but the replayability of it takes a massive hit. You can not play through an entire movie again on the same save file. This includes just purely playing the story again as well as in freeplay, neither lets you play through an entire movie again.

If what you want is to play through the story in lego form once and explore this game is great. But if what you want is that replayability of the other Lego games then go play them instead.

This isn't a bad game, you just got to know what you're buying.

Initially I did not like this game as Lego Star Wars 3 is goated. This game is fun in a vacuum, but its not perfect. It looks good but had some performance issues. It is a grind to 100% but felt more rewarding than other games. Worth it on sale, but otherwise play Lego Star Wars 3

tl;dr:
A fun, polished, very easy LEGO Star Wars game with great world exploration, decent combat, and a lot of charm, but also repetitive open-world content, weak traditional level design, underwhelming DLC, and some LEGO humor that undercuts the movies more than it celebrates them.

Score: 7/10
Recommendation: Buy it on discount.

Quick Verdict, buy this game if:
- You enjoy open-world exploration and collecting things.
- You want a chill, low-stress Star Wars game.
- You like LEGO humor and don’t mind the game constantly joking through the story.
- You want to wander around iconic Star Wars planets.
- You’re playing casually, especially with kids or family.

Maybe skip it if:
- You want a classic LEGO game structure.
- You care about strong, fully designed levels.
- You want meaningful combat depth or difficulty.
- You’re a completionist who hates repetitive collectible grinds.
- You want DLC that adds actual levels or gameplay.

Full Review:
LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga does not really feel like a classic LEGO game, but I do like the direction the developers went with. The biggest strength of the game is its open-world exploration. Walking around different planets, finding puzzles, collecting bricks, and messing around with different characters is genuinely fun.
That said, the exploration can get very repetitive. There are 1200 Kyber Bricks to collect, which sounds impressive until you realize how much of the game starts feeling like a checklist. Completionists will probably have a rough time with this one. Getting 100% is not satisfying so much as it is a genuine chore.
The actual story levels are also disappointing. Compared to older LEGO titles, the levels here often feel more like short mini-games than fully fleshed-out stages. They are usually quick, simple, and over before they really become interesting. If you are playing this game mainly to experience the Star Wars story, I would honestly recommend the older LEGO Star Wars games instead.

The Best Way to Play:
I’ve played through the game twice already, though I’ll admit I originally pirated it when it came out because I do not think any LEGO game should launch at €60. So my Steam playtime is not really accurate.
Personally, I think the best way to play this game is to pick the movies you want to go through, explore as much as you feel like between levels, and then stop once you have finished the final episode you wanted to play. Treat it like a casual Star Wars playground, not a game you need to fully complete. If you can let go of the urge to 100% everything, it becomes a much better experience.

Combat and Gameplay:
The combat feels sharp for about the first five minutes. Then you realize there is not much depth underneath it. There are flashy animations, lightsaber attacks, blaster combat, Force powers, combos, and unique character touches. On the surface, it looks great. But mechanically, most characters feel very similar once you understand the basic moveset. Every character having personal animations is a nice detail, and many team-ups have unique voice lines, but it also makes the lack of deeper combat feel like wasted potential. Still, it is fun to swing a lightsaber, throw things with the Force, shoot enemies, and fly around in ships. Space flight feels smooth and engaging at first, though like much of the game, it becomes repetitive fairly quickly. 

Performance:
The game runs very smoothly and does not require a high-end PC, which is definitely a plus. No complaints here.

My Two Biggest Criticisms:
1. The LEGO silliness goes too far. Older LEGO games knew when to make jokes and when to let a serious moment breathe. This game often does not. The jokes can be funny, but there are so many of them that they start to hurt the pacing. At times, it feels less like the game is lovingly parodying Star Wars and more like it is mocking it. This is especially noticeable with the sequel trilogy. No matter how you feel about those movies, the amount of ridicule aimed at them sometimes feels genuinely tasteless. LEGO used to be funny for both kids and adults while still clearly loving the source material. This game sometimes feels like it is making jokes in spite of the movies rather than because of them. The original trilogy does get more love, though.

2. The combat needed difficulty options. This game really could have benefited from difficulty settings or optional mechanics to make combat more engaging for older players. I know it is a kids’ game, so I am not expecting Dark Souls: LEGO Edition. But LEGO also knew plenty of adults were going to play this. A few optional mechanics could have made a huge difference: parrying, stamina or Force meters, limited lives in levels, stronger enemies, or more meaningful boss fights. The combat already looks flashy. It just does not ask much from the player. That is forgivable, but it still feels like wasted potential.

DLC: 
The DLC is very underwhelming. Since there is no character customization and the DLC mostly just adds more characters, it feels pretty pointless. No new levels, no meaningful gameplay additions, no real reason to care beyond “here are more characters.” They could have made something much more interesting, like a Rogue One story pack or Clone Wars levels. Instead, the DLC feels like easy money and not much else.

Final Thoughts:
Overall, I still recommend LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga, but with some clear warnings. It is not the best LEGO game, and it is not the best way to experience the Star Wars story. The traditional levels are weak, the combat is shallow, the humor can get exhausting, and 100%-ing the game is more chore than fun. But as a casual Star Wars sandbox? It works. Exploring planets, solving puzzles, unlocking characters, and messing around in the galaxy is still enjoyable. The game is polished, charming, easy to run, and fun in short bursts.

Final score: 7/10
Not the best LEGO game, but still a fun experience. Buy it on discount.

If you want good LEGO Star Wars game, go play Lego Star Wars III.
Story is okay-ish (come on, everybody knows the story at this point, it's just lego-fied here), but challenges will make sure you will lose your psyche. It doesn't even bring anything significant in new mechanics compared to other and older LEGO games. 
You will skim through story line in maybe 10 hours and spend next 90 (or more, I used exploits and skips to get to Kyber Bricks) to make it 100%. A lot of puzzles to get Bricks is same, jump around, time trial, shoot targets. Very few puzzles are unique. Side quests are okay, unless it's escort mission. why, you may ask? Because NPCs move faster than your walk speed and slower than your sprint speed. Lovely, right? 
And there are also side quests requiring moving through half of galaxy. Talk with one guy, go to another planet, talk with another guy, hunt something, pack your ship and come back to first guy, occasionally forcing out of hyperspace to engage in annoying fight with groups of enemy fighters. 

Overall, game is not some massive upgrade over other games, it has TON of grinding, a lot of stuff is repetitive, making whole experience boring. Can't recommend.
THE open world LEGO Star Wars game everybody was waiting for. So much fun, can't recommend enough!!!!!!
very fun i recoment for people who love mid to long games and grinding and lego and starwars

I will say this: The gameplay design is much better than in previous Lego games. You might need to get used to it if you use a keyboard and mouse, but all in all, it's like if Complete Saga had a new addition.

However, one of the things that I kinda dislike about this one is that it's too goofy for its own good and not really faithful to the original films. The Complete Saga is good for if you like screen accuracy, and this game is good if you want new gameplay design, enhanced graphics, and all nine episodes.

I still like it, it's just it kinda replaces the screen accuracy with goofiness a bit.

Score: 8/10

Transferred my game from the Epic, so I have more time played than 7 hours. The game isn't bad, it just isn't nearly as good as the original Lego Star Wars. You could easily get everything in the original and 100% in 24 hours while having a load of fun. This game supposedly takes around 100 hours, which I think is a little bit exaggerated, but still it's more mind numbing than fun. There was a really good formula for the older Lego games that players really miss. Everything doesn't HAVE to be open world with 10 trillion collectibles and get 100 quadrillion dollars. If it's on sale and you aren't a completionist then get it, if you are then good luck.
